strengths: Proven hands-on experience with agentic AI infrastructure and tooling, including contributions to MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks at re:cinq, directly aligning with the job's focus on modular, event-driven multi-agent systems

Deep expertise in distributed systems and real-time data processing, demonstrated through roles at Dunnhumby (low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec) and GISCollective (event-driven Geo-Data platform), which maps to the need for streaming data and real-time inference in AI-native fintech

Strong track record of scaling engineering practices and tooling, including setting up CI/CD pipelines, GitLab instances, and developer infrastructure at Altom Consulting, as well as founding and maintaining an open-source platform (GISCollective) with Kubernetes support, evidencing leadership in disciplined, production-focused engineering
concerns: Lacks explicit executive-level leadership experience (e.g., no CTO, VP, or Director titles), though roles like Staff Software Engineer at re:cinq and Altom Consulting suggest senior technical influence

No direct fintech infrastructure experience (e.g., credit systems, payment flows, or embedded finance), though adjacent work in real-time data and algorithmic optimization (e.g., pricing strategies at Optilyz) provides transferable skills

Limited evidence of hiring or developing senior technical leadership layers, a key requirement for scaling teams from 10 to 30+ engineers, though open-source leadership (GISCollective) and mentorship (workshops, talks) hint at potential

key_factors.factor: Agentic AI and distributed systems expertise

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo presents a compelling case for advancing due to his hands-on expertise in agentic AI tooling and event-driven architectures, directly addressing the job's core technical requirements. His background in scaling distributed systems (e.g., Dunnhumby's high-throughput microservices) and real-time data platforms (GISCollective) aligns closely with the need for AI-native fintech infrastructure. While lacking a CTO title, his leadership in open-source projects, developer tooling, and cross-functional engineering roles (e.g., Staff Engineer at re:cinq) demonstrates the ability to drive technical vision and operational rigor—key for scaling a high-performance engineering team. The absence of direct fintech experience is mitigated by adjacent work in algorithmic optimization (Optilyz) and regulated environments (e.g., compliance-aware design in GISCollective).
